notes: Loyal figure associated with Pyrrhus' time, known for offering to assassinate King Pyrrhus; mentioned in Roman historical sources.

docs.title: Roman History, Book Book 10
docs.author: Cassius Dio
docs.excerpt: … Nicias, one of those believed to be loyal to Pyrrhus, came to Fabricius and offered to assassinate the king. Fabricius, indignant at this, since he wished to overcome the enemy by valour and by main force, as Camillus had done, informed Pyrrhus of the plot.

docs.title: Life of Nicias
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … habits of living, in particular, his unsociable and exclusive ways, not like those of a fellow-citizen, or even a fellow-man, went against him, and having many times opposed their inclinations, forcing them against their feelings to do what was their interest, he had got himself disliked.
